rule_adherence.notes: Write path stays in main process behind typed IPC/contextBridge, debounce flushes at 500ms with no save button, write error is logged non-blocking, and preload is correctly compiled to CJS per the stack rules.

simplicity.notes: Debounce util and viewer wiring are minimal, but the storage cast plus a large stale 'BLOCKED — BUILD CONFIG' comment block in the E2E spec add noise.

spec_compliance.notes: Editable TipTap, debounced markdown round-trip write, Vitest fake-timer test, and Playwright spec are all present, but the E2E carries a self-described blocker note suggesting it may not pass as committed.

violations.rule: Be honest / spec: 'E2E (Playwright): ... assert file on disk changed' and 'All quality gates pass'
violations.evidence: BLOCKED — BUILD CONFIG ... Until then, this test will fail at the "Open folder" click step. — yet vite.config.ts already applies the CJS fix, leaving a misleading/stale comment whose verification status is unconfirmed.

violations.rule: Stateless / build hygiene — committed build artifacts
violations.evidence: compiled dist-electron output is committed alongside source, adding churn unrelated to the slice.

summary: Behaviourally complete and rule-compliant debounced-write slice, downgraded to concerns by a stale misleading E2E blocker comment and committed build artifacts.
